original text:
Since January 2004, all Canadian organizations engaged commercial activities have been required comply with the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act("PIPEDA") and the Canadian Standards Association Model Code for the Protection Personal Information incorporated reference into PIPEDA. These obligations extend lawyers and law firms, including Lawson Clark Oldman.
use your personal information provide legal advice and services you, issue invoices and maintain our database clients. addition, you apply for position with Lawson Clark Oldman, will use your personal information assess your candidacy. Lastly, may use your contact information (name, e-mail and postal address) that may communicate with you about recent developments the law, keep you abreast firm news and invite you our firm events.
You may withdraw your consent our collection, use and disclosure your personal information any time, subject legal and/or contractual restrictions and upon reasonable notice. Your withdrawal consent our collection, use and disclosure your personal information may impact our ability represent you and provide you with legal advice. You can ask not send you marketing communications following the opt-out instructions each communication you may let know contacting info@lcolaw.ca.
Lawson Clark Oldman uses various safeguards ensure that your personal information protected against loss, theft, misuse, unauthorized access, disclosure, copying alteration. These include: security our physical premises; our professional obligations; security software and firewalls prevent unauthorized computer access "hacking"; and internal passwords that restrict access our electronic files.
You have right challenge the accuracy and completeness your personal information and have amended, appropriate. You also have right request access your personal information and receive accounting how that information has been used and disclosed, subject certain exceptions prescribed law. For example, the requested information would reveal personal information about another individual, your request for access may limited denied. your request for access denied, Lawson Clark Oldman will notify you writing the reason for the denial.
request access amend your personal information, please contact the lawyer with whom you normally correspond.
Lawson Clark Oldman will respond inquiries about its policies and practices relating its handling your personal information. Inquiries should directed Lawson Clark Oldman. the complaint found justified, Lawson Clark Oldman will take appropriate measures resolve it, including, necessary, amending this Policy and its procedures.
you have any questions complaints about this Policy the handling your personal information, you wish withdraw your consent our use your personal information, request access update any information have file, please contact our firm.
